title: PagerDuty/Opsgenie Integration
dataSources: PagerDuty incidents API, Opsgenie alerts API
spl:
index=pagerduty sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| eval ack_time_min=round((acknowledged_at_epoch-created_at_epoch)/60,1)
| stats avg(ack_time_min) as avg_ack, avg(resolved_at_epoch-created_at_epoch)/60 as avg_resolve by service
description: Tracking alert lifecycle and on-call response metrics ensures incident response SLAs are met and identifies process improvements.
value: Tracking alert lifecycle and on-call response metrics ensures incident response SLAs are met and identifies process improvements.
implementation: Poll PagerDuty/Opsgenie API for incident data. Track acknowledgment time, resolution time, and escalation rates. Report on on-call workload distribution. Alert when acknowledgment SLA is breached.
visualization: Bar chart (MTTA by service), Line chart (incident volume trend), Table (open incidents), Single value (avg MTTA).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: PagerDuty API input.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: PagerDuty incidents API, Opsgenie alerts API.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll PagerDuty/Opsgenie API for incident data. Track acknowledgment time, resolution time, and escalation rates. Report on on-call workload distribution. Alert when acknowledgment SLA is breached.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pagerduty sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| eval ack_time_min=round((acknowledged_at_epoch-created_at_epoch)/60,1)
| stats avg(ack_time_min) as avg_ack, avg(resolved_at_epoch-created_at_epoch)/60 as avg_resolve by service
```

Understanding this SPL

**PagerDuty/Opsgenie Integration** — Tracking alert lifecycle and on-call response metrics ensures incident response SLAs are met and identifies process improvements.

Documented **Data sources**: PagerDuty incidents API, Opsgenie alerts API. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): PagerDuty API input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pagerduty; **sourcetype**: pagerduty:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pagerduty, sourcetype="pagerduty:incident". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_time_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by service** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm counts match the source or gateway for the same minutes; verify deduplication and routing explain any gap.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (MTTA by service), Line chart (incident volume trend), Table (open incidents), Single value (avg MTTA).
grandmaExplanation: We bring alerts and checks from the tools we already use so one place can show whether our coverage and hand-offs look healthy.
knownFalsePositives: Drills, handoffs, and duplicate rules can inflate counts. We dedupe in the system of record before we act twice.
